common.terms.title: Terms of Use (Licensee Portal)
common.terms.intro: These terms of use apply to the use of the internal provider/licensee portal (e.g. “DFSMANAGER”, hereinafter referred to as the “Portal”). They govern the relationship between the portal operator (“we”) and the registered licensees/providers (“you”).
common.terms.scopeText: These terms apply exclusively to portal use by licensees/providers (B2B). Separate terms apply to end customers/participants (booking trainings/camps) on the public website.
common.terms.servicesText1: The portal provides functions to create and manage offers/trainings, process bookings (e.g. confirm, cancel, terminate), manage customers, maintain content such as news/coach profiles, and generate documents/exports (e.g. PDF/CSV/DATEV export).
common.terms.servicesText2: A specific economic success (e.g. utilisation, revenue) is not owed.
common.terms.registrationText1: Use of the portal requires an activated account. Access data must be kept confidential and protected against unauthorised access. You are responsible for activities carried out via your account.
common.terms.registrationText2: Role and permission concepts (e.g. superadmin/licensee) determine which functions are visible and usable.
common.terms.dutiesText1: You are responsible for ensuring that the content you publish (e.g. offer descriptions, prices, dates, places, images, news) is correct, complete, and legally permissible.
common.terms.dutiesText2: You must ensure that you have the necessary usage rights (e.g. for images/logos/texts). Impermissible content (e.g. infringing, misleading, offensive) is prohibited.
common.terms.customerContractsText1: If you process bookings/services for end customers via the portal, the contract for the relevant training/course service is generally concluded between you and the end customer, unless expressly agreed otherwise.
common.terms.customerContractsText2: You are responsible for compliance with consumer information obligations, price information, cancellation/withdrawal rules (where applicable), and the proper performance of your services.
common.terms.availabilityText1: We strive for high availability of the portal. Temporary restrictions may occur due to maintenance, security updates, technical faults, or circumstances beyond our control.
common.terms.availabilityText2: We may adapt/develop functions further as long as the core purpose of the portal remains intact.
common.terms.supportText: Support channels (e.g. email) and response times may vary depending on the contract/plan. You are obliged to report disruptions as specifically as possible (steps, screenshots, timestamps).
common.terms.section.privacy: 8. Data use, privacy, data processing agreement (DPA)
common.terms.privacyText1: Personal data is processed in the portal (e.g. customer/booking data). Depending on the setup, you are the controller for this data, while we act as the processor.
common.terms.privacyText2BeforeLink: For this processing, a data processing agreement (DPA) is regularly required. Further information can be found in the
common.terms.securityText: It is prohibited to circumvent security mechanisms, access data without authorisation, transmit malicious code, perform automated access without permission, or use the portal in a way that impairs stability.
common.terms.feesText: If fees/subscriptions/commissions are agreed for portal use, the respective contractually agreed conditions apply. Without an express agreement, no claims arise.
common.terms.termText1: We may block accounts or deactivate content if there is justified suspicion of misuse, legal violations, or breaches of contract. Blocking may also occur for security reasons.
common.terms.termText2: Rules on termination (deadlines, data export, deletion) are governed by the respective agreement and legal requirements.
common.terms.liabilityText1: We are fully liable in cases of intent and gross negligence as well as for damage resulting from injury to life, body, or health.
common.terms.liabilityText2: In cases of slight negligence, we are only liable for breaches of essential contractual obligations and limited to the typically foreseeable damage.
common.terms.liabilityText3: You are responsible yourself for content, offers, price information, legal compliance of your publications, and the performance of your services towards end customers.
common.terms.confidentialityText: Non-public information (e.g. internal processes, export formats, admin workflows, prices) must be treated confidentially.
common.terms.finalText: German law applies. Place of jurisdiction is – where permitted – Duisburg. Should individual provisions be invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected.
common.terms.noteText: These terms of use apply exclusively to the licensee portal. Separate terms apply to the public website.
